title: Sedation recovery documentation
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

examination.text: Supports documenting recovery progress, baseline consciousness or function, vital monitoring, complications during recovery, information exchange, follow-up planning, and discharge advice.
examination.rationale: The BSG section supports monitoring until return to baseline and documented recovery only in gastrointestinal endoscopy sedation; it is not generalized to all sedation procedures.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports documenting recovery progress, baseline consciousness or function, vital monitoring, complications during recovery, information exchange, follow-up planning, and discharge advice.

source.title: British Society of Gastroenterology guidelines on sedation in gastrointestinal endoscopy

assessment.text: Supports documented oxygenation, ventilation, circulation, consciousness, temperature, pain or other recovery observations, and use of a recovery score where appropriate.
assessment.rationale: The ASA standard supports a documented recovery record and ongoing evaluation of oxygenation, ventilation, circulation, consciousness, temperature, pain, nausea, hydration, and other observed domains without auto-populating findings.
assessment.evidence_extract: Supports documented oxygenation, ventilation, circulation, consciousness, temperature, pain or other recovery observations, and use of a recovery score where appropriate.

exact_section.heading: Standard III — continual evaluation and recovery record

follow_up.text: Supports qualified discharge responsibility and institution-approved discharge criteria as recorded decisions, not automatic clearance.
follow_up.rationale: Discharge responsibility and criteria were reviewed, but no discharge decision or criteria are inferred from the generic legacy scaffold.
follow_up.evidence_extract: Supports qualified discharge responsibility and institution-approved discharge criteria as recorded decisions, not automatic clearance.

exact_section.heading: Standards IV–V — discharge responsibility and criteria

sources_used.title: British Society of Gastroenterology guidelines on sedation in gastrointestinal endoscopy

source_limitations: Procedure, sedation type, medicine, dose, oxygen, airway, respiratory and cardiovascular observations, consciousness, pain, nausea, temperature, events, intervention, response, baseline recovery, score, discharge decision, escort, instructions, and follow-up remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
